description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic krater vase with a wide, flared rim and two rounded handles on opposite sides. The exterior is decorated with classical Greek red-figure artwork, depicting human figures in draped garments engaged in various activities, possibly a ceremonial or festive scene. The body of the vase is predominantly a matte black with reddish-brown illustrations and a decorative band near the base. The handles are robust and integrated into the upper body, curving outward and then looping back to the vessel. The base is circular and tiered, providing stability and a sense of grandeur. The surface shows some signs of age and wear, but the painted motifs remain clear and visually striking.
description: A large, ancient Greek-style ceramic krater vase with red-figure artwork and two rounded handles, featuring detailed human motifs on a black and reddish-brown surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main painted scene with three draped human figures and a clear view of the wide rim and one handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the rounded handles and a partial figure, with the rim and base visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a different painted scene with multiple figures, including a woman holding a circular object, and both handles partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other handle and a partial view of the main painted scene, with the rim and base in profile.
attribute_pattern: classical human figures and geometric border
